_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about how important Country and Place is to the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples who are connected to the area where we live and learn.

_source.w: We are learning about how important Country and Place is to the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples who are connected to the area where we live and learn.
y: Understanding this connection helps us respect the deep ties Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples have to their land.
Country and Place is not just a location — it holds stories, laws, and spiritual meaning that have been passed down for thousands of years.
Learning about Country helps all of us think more deeply about our own connections to the land.
eg: Finding out which Aboriginal People are the Traditional Owners of the land where our school is located.
Listening to an Elder or community member share why Country is important to their people.
Looking at an Aboriginal languages map to identify the language group connected to our area.
Discussing how Country includes not just the land but also the water, sky, animals, and people.
Learning about the stories, songs, or art connected to the Country around our school.
Writing a class Acknowledgement of Country that shows genuine understanding and respect.
